item.title: India Inflation Inches Up as Iran War Drives Energy Costs
item.description: India’s inflation edged up last month as the war in the Middle East pushed up energy import costs.

item.title: Sea Tops Profit Estimates After Fending Off E-Commerce Rivals
item.description: Sea Ltd. reported quarterly earnings that topped analysts’ estimates after warding off rivals in Southeast Asia’s competitive e-commerce market rattled by economic and political challenges.

item.title: Australia Doomsday Scenario Sees Oil Hit $200 on War Escalation
item.description: Australia’s government conducted scenario analysis in its annual budget under which the war in Iran intensifies, driving oil to $200 a barrel and throwing the global economy into chaos.

item.title: Quiet Token Sales Boosted Trump Crypto Wealth by $660 Million
item.description: Trump family members made roughly $1.55 billion from sales of World Liberty Financial Inc. tokens, lifting their total fortune by about $660 million, after accounting for previously undisclosed transactions, according to the Bloomberg Billionaires Index.

item.title: EBay Rejects GameStop’s $56 Billion Takeover as Not Credible
item.description: EBay Inc. rejected a $56 billion takeover offer from GameStop Corp. Chief Executive Officer Ryan Cohen, calling the unsolicited bid “neither credible nor attractive” in a letter from Chairman Paul Pressler on Tuesday.

item.title: Bond Market Ditches Warsh Trade as Oil Surges
item.description: The bond market thought it had the Kevin Warsh trade figured out. Events changed that.

item.title: Business Confidence Sinks in Peru as Radical Candidate Nears Runoff
item.description: Peru’s chaotic presidential election is taking a toll on business sentiment as a radical candidate edges closer to a runoff following a prolonged vote count.

item.title: JD Profit Beats Estimates After China Curbs Food Delivery War
item.description: JD.com Inc. reported better-than-expected profit after Beijing clamped down on its fierce food delivery battle with Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. and Meituan.

item.title: Korea Benchmark Bond Yield Tops 4% as Rate-Hike Bets Grow
item.description: South Korea’s 10-year bond yield rose above 4% for the first time since late 2023, as an oil shock tied to the Iran conflict leads traders to expect bigger interest rate hikes.

item.title: Tele Columbus Creditors Unite Ahead of Potential Debt Talks
item.description: Creditors to Tele Columbus AG are banding together as the struggling German internet service provider controlled by Morgan Stanley Infrastructure Partners braces for debt talks.

item.title: Emerging-Market Currencies, Stocks Fall as Iran Peace Hopes Dim
item.description: Emerging-market currencies and stocks fell on concern the ceasefire between the US and Iran is on the brink of collapse, while a slump in South Korean shares also weighed on sentiment.

item.title: Australia Tackles Property Tax Breaks, Disappoints on Deficit
item.description: Australia will overhaul tax settings that have long favored property investors and assist households and firms grappling with soaring fuel costs in a budget that outlined deficits across the forecast horizon.

item.title: Siemens Said to Mull Acquisition of Rail Technology Firm Mer Mec
item.description: Siemens AG is exploring a potential acquisition of Mer Mec SpA, an Italian maker of signaling and communications equipment for trains, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: ECB’s Patsalides Says Things Pointing to June Rate Hike: MNI
item.description: The European Central Bank may need to increase borrowing costs at its June meeting because of heightened inflation risks, Governing Council member Christodoulos Patsalides told MNI.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Lakos-Bujas Says Profits Eclipse War Risks for Stocks
item.description: Strong corporate earnings are currently outweighing geopolitical concerns in financial markets despite tensions between the US and Iran, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s Dubravko Lakos-Bujas.

item.title: Hungary to Meet Euro Criteria by 2030, New Finance Minister Says
item.description: Hungary’s next finance minister pledged to meet all criteria for euro adoption within four years as part of a “radical” economic policy shift after Viktor Orban’s 16-year rule.

item.title: Bond Market’s Warsh Trade Falls Apart as Oil Fans Inflation Risk
item.description: Not long ago, the Treasury market thought it had the Kevin Warsh trade figured out: Simply bet on the multiple interest rate cuts that the nominee had been expected to deliver if he got the job to lead the Federal Reserve.

item.title: German Investor Outlook Unexpectedly Improves on Hope Over War
item.description: German investor optimism unexpectedly improved on hope that the fighting in the Middle East will end soon, reducing the threat to Europe’s largest economy.

item.title: Pop Mart Sales Growth Decelerates Amid Sharp Overseas Slowdown
item.description: Pop Mart International Group Ltd. reported slower sales growth for the first quarter, marking a deceleration from its rapid expansion last year amid waning momentum for its blockbuster Labubu toys.

item.title: German Firms’ Outlook in China Improves Despite Geopolitics
item.description: German businesses’ outlook on the Chinese economy improved from last year despite the Iran war impacting their operations and persistent geopolitical tensions.

item.title: South Africa Joblessness Rises Before Full Iran-War Impact Felt
item.description: South Africa’s jobless rate increased by more than expected with losses seen in a number of sectors including construction and community and social services, putting the labor market on a weaker footing even as the Iran war got underway.

item.title: China’s ‘Invoice Economy’ Crackdown Leaves Copper Trade Reeling
item.description: At Asia’s biggest metal industry gathering last week, a single word dominated almost every conversation — fapiao, China’s humble tax receipt.

item.title: Canada Eyes Free Trade Pacts With Philippines, Asean This Year
item.description: Canada expects to conclude free trade agreements with the Philippines and the wider Southeast Asian bloc this year, as Ottawa seeks to boost business ties with the region and grow its non-US trade.

item.title: Qatar Asks Ships to Go Dark at LNG Port in New Safety Measure
item.description: Qatar is asking ships at its main liquefied natural gas export facility to turn off their transponders, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Telenor Sells Stake in Internet-of-Things Business to Verdane
item.description: Telenor ASA agreed to sell a stake in its internet-of-things venture to Verdane Group as the telecommunications company overhauls operations to focus on the core Nordic business.

item.title: Mitsubishi Heavy Sees Global Gas Turbine Demand Staying Strong
item.description: Global orders for gas turbines will “decline slightly” this year from 2025, but remain strong due to the build-out of data centers, according to Mitsubishi Heavy Industries Ltd., a major manufacturer of the equipment used in power plants.

item.title: Shiseido May Swap Oil for Plants on Cosmetic Supply Chain Chaos
item.description: Shiseido Co. is looking to potentially swap out oil-based inputs for plant-derived materials as the Middle East conflict upends the global skincare and cosmetics supply chain.

item.title: China Earns $500 Million an Hour From Exports Supercharged by AI
item.description: The US and Chinese economies may still be on their way to decoupling but both are drawing strength from the same source.

item.title: ECB Must Act If Iran War Threatens Price Stability, Nagel Says
item.description: The European Central Bank will continue analyzing the economy before deciding next month whether to raise interest rates, but policymakers must take action if the Iran war jeopardizes price stability, according to Governing Council member Joachim Nagel.

item.title: Russia Cuts Growth Outlook Despite Putin’s Push to Rally Economy
item.description: Russia cut its official growth forecast for this year, even after President Vladimir Putin signaled frustration with the slowdown and ordered the government to respond.
